name: Sauteed Breast of Chicken a La King
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces Domestic white mushrooms, sliced thin (1 cup)
1 small Red bell pepper, roasted, peeled, and seeded OR 1 jar (2 ounces) drained roasted red bell pepper, cut into medium dice (1/2 cup)
1/2 cup Green bell pepper, cut into medium dice
1/2 cup Chicken stock or canned chicken broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the chicken breasts with 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper. Dust lightly with flour.
2. Heat the oil and 2 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet. Add the chicken breasts; saute until lightly browned on both sides and cooked through, about 8 minutes. Remove the chicken from the skillet, cover, and keep warm. Discard the oil and butter from the skillet.
3. Heat the remaining butter in the skillet. Add the mushrooms, red and green peppers, and scallions; saute for about 3 minutes until the vegetables are softened.
4. Add the sherry; stir to loosen browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Add the chicken stock and simmer until the liquid reduces to 1/4 cup, about 7 minutes.
5. Mix the cornstarch and heavy cream together; add to the skillet. Bring to a boil and simmer until the sauce thickens, about 2 minutes.
6. Return the chicken breasts to the skillet and simmer them to heat through, about 1 minute.
7. To serve, place a chicken breast on each warmed dinner plate and spoon a portion of the sauce over each chicken breast. Sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately.
8. Makes 4 servings.
